design:
DATA FLOW (extends the existing per-camera worker -> Event pipeline; nothing in the live/recording path changes shape):

1. camera_worker._safe_detect already yields YOLO person Boxes (xyxy, score) per processed frame. Today only an aggregate "person trigger" fires an Event. We add a per-detection identification step that runs on the SAME trigger frame the worker already copies (snapshot in _record_and_persist), so we pay no extra decode cost and stay within the existing detect_every_n / detect_interval throttle.

2. For each person Box on the trigger frame, a new IdentityPipeline (app/reid/pipeline.py) produces a "sighting feature":
   - crop = snapshot[y1:y2, x1:x2]
   - APPEARANCE embedding: ReIDEmbedder (app/reid/embedder.py, OSNet ONNX, onnxruntime-gpu) on the full body crop -> 512-d L2-normalized float32. Always computed (back turned / masked still gives a body vector).
   - FACE embedding: reuse the existing insightface FaceRecognizer. Run it on the crop (or on the whole frame and assign the largest face whose center falls inside the box). When a face with det_score >= face_det_thresh and a min pixel size is found -> 512-d ArcFace vector; else None.
   - Each crop yields a SightingFeature{box, appearance_vec, face_vec|None, face_det_score, crop_quality}.

3. IdentityManager (app/reid/manager.py) is the brain. It runs IN-PROCESS in each camera worker (like FaceIndex does today: derived state rebuilt from the DB at worker start) but writes through to the DB as the single source of truth. For each SightingFeature it does online assignment:
   - Match against the in-memory identity gallery (face gallery = FAISS IndexFlatIP over identity-level face centroids/exemplars; appearance gallery = recent per-identity appearance exemplars with timestamps).
   - Decide MATCH (existing identity) vs NEW (auto-create "Person N") using the fusion rules in matching_algorithm.
   - Persist a Sighting row (camera_id, ts, identity_id, bbox, det_score, has_face, scores, crop thumb path) and update the identity's running face/appearance exemplars (FaceExemplar / AppearanceExemplar rows + in-memory centroids). Cross-camera linking is automatic because every worker rebuilds the same gallery from the shared DB and re-syncs periodically.

4. The existing Event is enriched: when the trigger frame's dominant person is identified, the worker attaches identity_id (+ denormalized name + the fused match score) onto the Event. Event keeps working exactly as before when ReID is disabled or unavailable (graceful degradation, same as faces today).

5. Periodic background maintenance (app/reid/maintenance.py, a thread started in main.lifespan): time-decays appearance exemplars, prunes stale/low-quality exemplars, recomputes identity centroids + representative thumbnail, and runs a conservative offline merge pass (face-based) to repair over-segmentation. The API process owns the authoritative gallery; workers reload it on a timer.

6. UI: a new "Identities" tab (auto-discovered identities) is added to the SPA, backed by a new /api/identities router, with rename / merge / split operator tools. Existing "People" (manual face DB) tab is left intact; identities are the automatic layer.

COEXISTENCE WITH FACES: ArcFace is reused as-is (no second face model). Only the OSNet appearance model is new on the GPU (~30-60MB fp16, see model_plan), keeping total added VRAM well under budget.
db_changes:
All new ORM models go in app/db/models.py (owned by the integration component). They inherit the EXISTING models.Base, so init_db's models.Base.metadata.create_all auto-creates them on next boot — no Alembic needed for NEW tables. Existing-table COLUMN additions are handled by a tiny idempotent ALTER-TABLE shim (see below) because create_all never alters existing tables in SQLite.

NEW TABLES (auto-created via create_all):

1. identities  -- one row per auto-discovered person
   - id PK
   - name (str, default e.g. "Person {id}"; operator-renamable)
   - is_named (bool, default False) -> True once an operator renames/confirms; protects from auto-merge
   - notes (str|None)
   - rep_sighting_id (int|None, FK sightings.id ON DELETE SET NULL) -> representative thumbnail
   - face_centroid (LargeBinary|None) -> 512 f32, running mean of face exemplars, L2-normalized (derived cache)
   - appearance_centroid (LargeBinary|None) -> 512 f32, time-decayed mean of appearance exemplars (derived cache)
   - num_sightings (int, default 0; denormalized counter)
   - first_seen (datetime), last_seen (datetime)
   - created_at (server_default now())
   - relationships: sightings, face_exemplars, appearance_exemplars (cascade all,delete-orphan, passive_deletes)

2. sightings  -- one row per identified person-detection
   - id PK
   - identity_id FK identities.id ON DELETE CASCADE, index
   - camera_id FK cameras.id ON DELETE CASCADE, index
   - event_id FK events.id ON DELETE SET NULL, nullable, index  (links the sighting to the triggered Event/clip when there is one)
   - ts (datetime, index)
   - bbox_x1/y1/x2/y2 (int)
   - det_score (float)
   - has_face (bool)
   - face_score (float|None)  -> cosine to matched identity face centroid
   - appearance_score (float|None)
   - match_kind (str: 'face' | 'appearance' | 'new')
   - thumb_path (str|None) -> cropped body thumbnail data/identities/<identity_id>/<sighting_id>.jpg
   - created_at
   - composite Index ix_sightings_identity_ts (identity_id, ts); Index ix_sightings_camera_ts (camera_id, ts)

3. face_exemplars  -- representative ArcFace vectors kept per identity (cap ~8, quality-pruned)
   - id PK, identity_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE index
   - vector (LargeBinary, 512 f32 L2-normalized)
   - det_score (float), camera_id (int|None), sighting_id (int|None FK ON DELETE SET NULL)
   - created_at

4. appearance_exemplars  -- per-identity OSNet vectors with timestamps for time-decay (cap ~16, decayed/pruned)
   - id PK, identity_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE index
   - vector (LargeBinary, 512 f32 L2-normalized)
   - quality (float), camera_id (int|None), sighting_id (int|None FK ON DELETE SET NULL)
   - ts (datetime, index)  -> the capture time used by the decay function
   - created_at

EXISTING-TABLE COLUMN ADD:
   - events.identity_id (int|None, FK identities.id ON DELETE SET NULL, index) -> links a triggered Event to its identity (separate from the legacy manual person_id, which stays untouched for the manual face DB).

MIGRATION APPROACH: integration adds a function ensure_reid_schema(engine) invoked from init_db AFTER create_all. It (a) lets create_all build the four new tables, then (b) runs idempotent "ALTER TABLE events ADD COLUMN identity_id INTEGER REFERENCES identities(id)" guarded by a PRAGMA table_info check (SQLite can't add an FK constraint to an existing table, so identity_id is added as a plain INTEGER column with the relationship declared at the ORM level; ON DELETE behavior for this denormalized link is handled in application code on identity delete, mirroring how delete_person already nulls Event.person_id explicitly). create_all is safe and idempotent for the new tables; existing tables are only ever touched by the guarded ADD COLUMN.
matching_algorithm:
Online per-sighting assignment, computed against the in-memory gallery (face FAISS IndexFlatIP over face exemplars; appearance candidates filtered by a time/space window). All vectors L2-normalized so inner product == cosine.

THRESHOLDS (config-driven, defaults tuned for buffalo_l ArcFace + OSNet on a T4):
  - FACE_MATCH = 0.42 (strong accept; ArcFace buffalo_l cosine). FACE_STRONG = 0.55 (auto-accept even if appearance disagrees).
  - FACE_REJECT_NEW = 0.32 (below this, face provides no evidence).
  - APP_MATCH = 0.62 (OSNet cosine accept when used as the primary link).
  - APP_GATE = 0.50 (appearance corroboration floor when fused with a borderline face).
  - APP_WINDOW_SECONDS = 600 (10 min): appearance-only links allowed only within this temporal window of an identity's last appearance exemplar.
  - APP_SPACE: no hard geometry (cameras uncalibrated) but appearance-only links across DIFFERENT cameras require a slightly higher bar (APP_MATCH_CROSS = 0.66) and APP_WINDOW_SECONDS to bound teleportation.
  - MIN_FACE_PIXELS = 24 (skip tiny faces for matching/exemplars). MIN_APP_BOX_AREA frac = 0.01 of frame, MIN_ASPECT for a plausible standing/sitting body (reject slivers).

DECISION ORDER (per SightingFeature):
  1. If face_vec present and good (det_score >= det_thresh, >= MIN_FACE_PIXELS):
       face_hit = best identity by face cosine.
       - if face_score >= FACE_STRONG -> ASSIGN to face_hit (match_kind='face'). Authoritative; ignore appearance.
       - elif face_score >= FACE_MATCH -> ASSIGN to face_hit (match_kind='face').
       - elif FACE_REJECT_NEW <= face_score < FACE_MATCH:
           borderline. Look up appearance candidates in window; if the same identity also has appearance_score >= APP_GATE -> ASSIGN (fused). Else treat face as weak -> fall through to appearance, then NEW.
       - else (face_score < FACE_REJECT_NEW): face says "not these" -> fall through to appearance, then NEW.
  2. Appearance fallback (no usable face, OR weak face fell through):
       Gather candidate identities whose most-recent appearance exemplar ts is within APP_WINDOW_SECONDS. Score = time-decayed cosine (see decay). 
       - if best appearance_score >= (APP_MATCH same-camera | APP_MATCH_CROSS cross-camera) AND the candidate's face centroid does NOT strongly contradict (no face this sighting, so no contradiction) -> ASSIGN (match_kind='appearance').
       - else -> NEW.
  3. NEW: create Identity name="Person {next}", seed its first face/appearance exemplar, match_kind='new'.

APPEARANCE TIME-DECAY: when scoring an appearance candidate, weight each stored appearance exemplar by exp(-Δt / TAU) with TAU = 12h (configurable). effective_score = max over exemplars of (cosine * decay_weight); an exemplar older than ~2*TAU contributes negligibly. This encodes "people change clothes between days": yesterday's outfit barely links today. The identity's appearance_centroid is the decay-weighted mean, recomputed lazily/in maintenance. Face exemplars are NOT decayed (faces are time-stable).

EXEMPLAR UPDATE ON MATCH:
  - Face: if face_score in a "useful but not redundant" band (e.g. 0.45..0.9) and identity has < MAX_FACE_EXEMPLARS (8), append a FaceExemplar; else if full, replace the lowest-quality. Update face_centroid (running normalized mean). High-quality diverse exemplars beat a single centroid for pose/lighting robustness.
  - Appearance: append AppearanceExemplar with ts; cap MAX_APP_EXEMPLARS (16) using a quality+recency eviction (drop oldest-lowest). Recompute appearance_centroid (decay-weighted).

ANTI-EXPLOSION SAFEGUARDS:
  - Hysteresis / debounce: the worker already debounces triggers (min_trigger_frames, trigger_cooldown). Within an active person streak, keep a short-lived per-track "sticky" assignment (last identity for an overlapping box, IoU >= 0.3, within 2s) so the same continuous person is not re-evaluated into a new identity every frame.
  - Quality gating: only crops passing MIN_APP_BOX_AREA/aspect and (for face exemplars) MIN_FACE_PIXELS can CREATE a new identity or add exemplars. A blurry/occluded crop that matches nothing is logged as an unassigned sighting rather than spawning an identity (configurable: REQUIRE_QUALITY_FOR_NEW=True).
  - New-identity rate limit: per camera, cap new-identity creation (e.g. <= N per minute) to survive detector noise bursts; excess unmatched crops are dropped, not enrolled.
  - Provisional identities: a NEW identity with only 1 low-evidence sighting and no face is marked provisional; maintenance deletes provisional identities that never accrue a 2nd sighting or a face within a grace period (e.g. 10 min).

ANTI-WRONG-MERGE SAFEGUARDS:
  - Face is the only signal allowed to MERGE two existing identities; appearance never auto-merges (clothes are shared/ambiguous). Appearance only links a fresh sighting to ONE candidate, and only within the time window.
  - Face contradiction veto: if a sighting has a confident face matching identity A (>= FACE_MATCH) it can never be appearance-assigned to identity B, even if appearance to B is high.
  - is_named identities are frozen against automatic merges (operator intent wins). Auto-merge in maintenance only merges two AUTO identities when their face centroids cosine >= FACE_MERGE = 0.6 AND they have no time-overlapping sightings on different cameras (can't be the same person in two places at once) — conservative, face-only.
  - Margin test: accept a match only if best_score - second_best_score >= MARGIN (face 0.06, appearance 0.05) to avoid ambiguous ties; ambiguous -> NEW (safer to over-segment than to wrong-merge; operator MERGE fixes over-segmentation, SPLIT fixes wrong-merge).

OPERATOR CORRECTIONS feed back: MERGE reassigns all sightings/exemplars of B into A and recomputes centroids; SPLIT re-clusters a chosen identity's sightings (by face when available, else appearance) into two identities. Both are face-priority and update the gallery + the in-memory worker galleries on next reload.

components.name: reid-embedder
components.responsibility: OSNet appearance Re-ID model wrapper: load the exported ONNX under onnxruntime-gpu (CUDA EP -> CPU fallback, fp16), preprocess a BGR person crop (resize 128x256, ImageNet normalize), run inference, return a 512-d L2-normalized float32 appearance embedding. Mirrors the structure/conventions of app/faces/recognizer.py (lazy load, provider selection, process-local singleton getter). Includes a crop-quality helper (size/aspect/blur) and the model download/export script. Self-contained: imported lazily by the worker via the pipeline.

components.name: reid-identity-core
components.responsibility: The identity gallery + matching/clustering engine and the sighting feature extraction orchestration, all DB-backed (single source of truth) and mirroring app/faces/index.py conventions. Owns: IdentityGallery (face FAISS IndexFlatIP over face exemplars + appearance candidate store with time-decay, rebuild_from_db, periodic reload), IdentityManager.assign(sighting_feature)->(identity_id, match_kind, scores) implementing the full fusion/threshold/anti-explosion algorithm, exemplar update/eviction, NEW-identity creation, decay math, and the conservative offline merge/split helpers used by the API and maintenance. IdentityPipeline.extract(frame, boxes, face_recognizer, reid_embedder)->[SightingFeature]. Pure logic over injected embedders + a DB session; no edits to shared files.

components.name: reid-maintenance
components.responsibility: Background maintenance worker (a thread started from the integration-owned main.lifespan, but its logic lives here): periodic appearance time-decay + exemplar pruning, identity centroid + representative-thumbnail recompute, provisional-identity cleanup, new-identity rate-limit bookkeeping, and the conservative face-only auto-merge pass. Exposes start(app_state)/stop() and run_once(session) so it is unit-testable and so the integration component just calls into it.

components.name: identities-api
components.responsibility: New FastAPI router mounted at /api/identities (NEW file, no edits to existing routers): list identities (with rep thumbnail url, num_sightings, first/last seen, cameras seen on), get one identity + its sightings (paginated, cross-camera/time), serve sighting body-crop thumbnails, and operator tools RENAME (PUT name + set is_named), MERGE (POST /merge {source_ids,target_id}), SPLIT (POST /{id}/split {sighting_ids|auto}). Delegates merge/split to reid-identity-core helpers and triggers a gallery reload. Adds its own Pydantic schemas in a NEW schemas module to avoid touching app/schemas.py.

components.name: identities-ui
components.responsibility: Frontend Identities gallery as SELF-CONTAINED JS/CSS modules loaded by index.html (the single <script>/<link> wiring is done by the integration component). Renders the auto-discovered identities grid (representative thumbnails, counts, cameras), an identity detail view (all sightings across cameras/time with thumbnails and links to the source Event clip), and operator controls for rename/merge/split calling /api/identities. Vanilla JS matching app.js conventions (el(), api layer pattern).

components.name: integration
components.responsibility: Owns ALL edits to existing shared files so parallel work never conflicts. Wires the new tables + events.identity_id column into models.py and the migration shim into database.init_db; adds all REID_* settings to config.py; integrates the per-detection identification step into camera_worker.py (build ReIDEmbedder + IdentityManager in _build_components, run IdentityPipeline + IdentityManager.assign in the trigger path, persist sightings, attach identity_id/name/score to the Event) AND fixes the existing face recognizer/index call mismatch in the worker while there (recognizer constructed with the real signature, FaceRecognizer.detect/embedding access, FaceIndex.match); marshals new config into the worker cfg dict in manager.py; boots the identity gallery + maintenance thread in main.lifespan and mounts the identities router; adds the Identities tab + script/style includes to index.html and the nav hook in app.js; updates Dockerfile/pyproject (torch-free ONNX runtime already present; add the OSNet model to download path) and the model download script wiring.

shared_file_edits:
ALL edits below are assigned to the single 'integration' component:

- app/db/models.py: add 4 new ORM models (Identity, Sighting, FaceExemplar, AppearanceExemplar) inheriting the existing models.Base; add Event.identity_id mapped column + Event<->Identity relationship; add cascade/index declarations as specified in db_changes.

- app/db/database.py: in init_db, after models.Base.metadata.create_all(engine), call a new ensure_reid_schema(engine) that runs the idempotent guarded ALTER TABLE events ADD COLUMN identity_id (PRAGMA table_info check). Keep WAL/FK pragmas as-is.

- app/config.py: add REID settings to Settings with defaults — reid_enabled(bool=True), reid_model("osnet_x0_25_msmt17.onnx"), reid_input_w(128), reid_input_h(256), reid_embedding_dim(512), reid_device(alias of device), and all thresholds/windows/caps from matching_algorithm (face_match, face_strong, face_reject_new, app_match, app_match_cross, app_gate, app_window_seconds, app_decay_tau_seconds, max_face_exemplars, max_app_exemplars, new_identity_rate_per_min, provisional_grace_seconds, face_merge_threshold, match_margin_face, match_margin_app, min_face_pixels, min_app_box_area_frac). Add reid_model_path property and identities_dir property (data_dir/'identities'); include it in ensure_dirs().

- app/workers/camera_worker.py: in _build_components, also construct ReIDEmbedder (app.reid.embedder) and IdentityManager (app.reid.manager) when reid_enabled, degrading gracefully like faces. In the trigger path (_record_and_persist), after building the thumbnail, run IdentityPipeline.extract over the trigger-frame boxes + IdentityManager.assign per crop, persist Sighting rows + crop thumbs, pick the dominant box's identity, and pass identity_id/identity_name/identity_score into _update_event_row. ALSO fix the pre-existing face call mismatch here: construct FaceRecognizer with its real signature (models_dir/pack_name/device/det_size), use recognizer.detect() + DetectedFace.embedding, and FaceIndex.match(vec, threshold=...). Add a lightweight per-track sticky cache for the hysteresis safeguard.

- app/workers/manager.py: extend _camera_config to marshal the new reid_* tunables (model path, thresholds, windows, caps, reid_enabled, identities_dir) into the picklable worker cfg dict.

- app/main.py: in lifespan, boot the IdentityGallery (rebuild_from_db) onto app.state.identity_gallery and start the reid-maintenance thread; add 'app.api.identities' to _OPTIONAL_ROUTERS so it is mounted defensively; stop the maintenance thread on shutdown.

- app/static/index.html: add an 'Identities' tab button + a view-identities <section>, and include identities.js / identities.css.

- app/static/app.js: register the 'identities' view in the views map + nav so switchView routes to the identities-ui render/teardown (the render/teardown impls live in identities.js).

- app/static/style.css: add minimal layout hooks if needed (most identity styling lives in identities.css).

- pyproject.toml: no new heavy deps required (onnxruntime-gpu, opencv, numpy already present). Add a comment noting the OSNet ONNX is a model asset, not a Python dep. (torch/torchreid are only needed offline at export time, NOT in the runtime image.)

- Dockerfile: add /app/data/identities to the mkdir list so the image runs without mounts.

- scripts/download_models.py: add an OSNet step that fetches the pre-exported ONNX (or invokes scripts/export_reid_onnx.py) into models/, idempotent like the existing yolo/insightface steps.
model_plan:
MODEL: OSNet osnet_x0_25 trained on MSMT17 (Torchreid model zoo key 'osnet_x0_25', pretrained 'osnet_x0_25_msmt17'). Rationale: x0_25 is the lightest OSNet (~0.2M params), MSMT17 generalizes better across cameras than Market-1501. Output is the 512-d global feature (the layer before the classifier), which is what we L2-normalize and store. If accuracy is insufficient in field testing, the drop-in upgrade is osnet_ain_x1_0 (AIN = better cross-domain), same export path and same 512-d output; config.reid_model just points at the other file.

INPUT: BGR person crop -> RGB, resize to 128x256 (WxH, the torchreid ReID standard), scale to [0,1], normalize with ImageNet mean/std (mean [0.485,0.456,0.406], std [0.229,0.224,0.225]), NCHW float (or float16 for the fp16 export). EMBEDDING DIM: 512, L2-normalized -> cosine via inner product, consistent with the FaceIndex/FAISS convention already in the repo.

HOW TO OBTAIN / EXPORT (offline, on a dev box with torch — NOT in the runtime image), implemented by scripts/export_reid_onnx.py:
  pip install torch torchreid onnx
  import torchreid, torch
  model = torchreid.models.build_model('osnet_x0_25', num_classes=1, pretrained=True)  # downloads MSMT17 weights
  torchreid.utils.load_pretrained_weights(model, 'osnet_x0_25_msmt17')  # or use the zoo URL
  model.eval()
  # ensure forward returns the feature embedding (set model.training=False; torchreid returns features in eval)
  dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, 256, 128)
  torch.onnx.export(model, dummy, 'models/osnet_x0_25_msmt17.onnx', input_names=['images'], output_names=['features'], dynamic_axes={'images': {0: 'batch'}, 'features': {0: 'batch'}}, opset_version=12)
  # optional fp16: convert with onnxconverter-common float16.convert_float_to_float16 for ~half the size/VRAM and faster T4 inference.
The runtime container needs ONLY the resulting .onnx + onnxruntime-gpu (already a dependency); torch/torchreid never ship in the image. download_models.py either pulls a pre-exported ONNX from a release asset (idempotent, like yolov8n.onnx) or runs the export script if torch is present.

VRAM / PERF on the T4 (Turing, fp16/int8 ok, no bf16/FP8): osnet_x0_25 fp16 ONNX session is roughly 30-60 MB of VRAM and sub-millisecond-to-~2ms per crop at 128x256; even with several people per trigger frame this is negligible next to YOLOv8n + buffalo_l, keeping total added GPU footprint far under the ~2.5 GB ceiling. Batch the per-frame crops into a single inference call (dynamic batch axis exported above) to amortize launch overhead. CUDAExecutionProvider with CPUExecutionProvider fallback, exactly like the existing YOLO/face recognizers, so a GPU-contended box degrades to CPU instead of crashing the worker.
